observed_facts: One inbound lead form event was processed for submission FORM-LEAD-2001.
One runtime execution reported success for the same correlation identity.
The first CRM create outcome was uncertain but destination_committed was true.
The second CRM create was confirmed and also committed.
Both CRM creates had a null idempotency key.
Final external state contained two CRM leads and one notification for the submission.
primary_hypothesis: The workflow retried CRM creation after an uncertain but committed write without business-level idempotency or destination reconciliation, creating a duplicate lead.
alternative_hypotheses: The CRM integration may not enforce uniqueness unless the caller supplies an idempotency key.
The workflow may deduplicate inbound events but not outbound CRM writes.
The workflow may classify an uncertain outcome as a failed write and immediately attempt another create.
missing_evidence: Workflow source and node-level execution logs are unavailable.
Raw CRM request and response records are unavailable.
The CRM idempotency and uniqueness semantics are unknown.
recommended_next_investigation: Inspect the CRM create path to verify whether uncertain outcomes trigger a destination lookup before another write and whether a stable idempotency key is derived from submission identity.
